Con: Once the ice is dropped, you want to get it into the freezer. Your new ice will begin to melt together and become one big cube if you leave it go too long. It definitely deserves 4 stars, we love the machine!! If you need a ton of ice, begin early or use backup methods. ENJOY!!

clean_review_title: High quality item at a great price
clean_review_text: This is a VERY nice wallet. My husband is still carrying around the same wallet that hes had for probably 8 years now, and it looks ROUGH. So I've been trying to find him one that he will use. I have been really happy to see the RFID blocking capability available more now and this has proved to be an excellent option for him. The first thing you notice about this wallet right off the bat is how it feels and looks. It really looks like a high quality leather product and not some cheap option. The stitching is very neat and professional and the whole things feels pretty substantial (the way true leather should) even though its much slimmer than most traditional wallets. The pockets are the perfect size to allow credit cards to slip in and out without too much resistance, and the leather is sticky enough to keep them in even if you hold it upside down. The ID holder is big enough that you dont have to remove the ID whenever it needs to be seen, and the pockets behind the card holder and ID holder are big enough to hold a little cash, receipts, or business cards. This is definitely streamlined, as it is only big enough to hold the essentials, but if you dont have much to carry around, this wallet is perfect. My husband has been very pleased with it so far. I did receive this item at a reduced cost and/or free in exchange for my honest and unbiased review, but this item is well worth paying full price for, in my opinion. It's a very nice wallet.

clean_review_text: I have worn contacts for 20 years now. Wow! And tried many lense brands from acuvue oasis, air optic, acuvue 2, dailies, freshlook colored (teenage years). You get the picture I have sampled a few. Also with that I have probably tried every solution available. Opti-free, bio true, complete, re-nu. It wasnt until a optometrist appointment about 6 years ago I was introduced to this stuff. The doctor said I had an "eye allery" and advised I should switch to this cleaner. I do not believe I had said "eye allergy" or needed the prescription medication I took. But I did and bought this while checking out with my medicine. I havent used another brand since. This stuff is great! But needs to be used with care. I have had my fair share of red eye situations...it is painful. In my experience when I have had this happen, it was my own innocence that got me there. This product has a great instruction manual and covers almost everything. What they dont say is if you over fill past the line, the poroxide with bubble out of the top pin hole all over your case. Think of a volcano erupting. The lava running out the top.and down the sides. The same happens if the case is too full. So when you unscrew the case you have essentially coated those fingers in poroxide. And if your like me, those same fingers are used to insert your contacts. So you sre basically putting poroxide onto your contact and into your eye. Make sure to discard the solution in the case as soon as you have removed your contacts. Do not reuse. The product is great and it allows me to wear my acuvue lenses for 2 weeks comfortably. They feel like a fresh pair after every cleaning. I will only use.thid solution for sanitizing my contact lenses.

product_title: Bosch SHE3AR55UC Ascenta 24" Stainless Steel Full Console Dishwasher - Energy Star
clean_review_title: If I could, I would get something else
clean_review_text: Had to replace the dishwasher in a new house. Looked into many models, and decided to go with this model (bought at a local BestBuy). Used for 5 months prior to writing this review. Pros: 1. Washes dishes well. 2. Supposedly saves water (not sure if I can check this claim, so I am taking it as is) 3. Supposedly saves energy cause it's not using dry-heat at the end of the cycle but rather FINISH rinse agent (more on it in #3 of Cons) 4. Nice looks and easy to operate. Mainly use the AUTO wash - set it and forget it. Also we use 3hr delay so we don't get bothered with noise (later about the noise). Cons: 1. It is much smaller than other dishwashers we have owned and/or operated (primarily Kenmoore and Whirlpool). I think this Bosch's basic model was made smaller on purpose to make you buy a more advanced washer that is sized properly. For a family of 3, we turn the dishwasher on at least every other day, sometomes daily (when we have guests and such) 2. Maybe I got a defective dishwasher, but mine is NOT QUITE. Again, I compare with kenmoores and whirlpools that I have used and/or owned. We set the 3-hr delay so that in the evening we can watch the TV in the living room without having to turn the volume up. 3. This dishwasher goes though FINISH [[ASIN:B0026WRHK6 Finish Jet-dry Rinse Agent, Original Liquid, 32 Oz]] lightning fast. We set the use of FINISH to the lowest, but the LOW indicator lights up every three or so weeks. Maybe the dishwasher is saving water (see #2 in pros above) and energy (see #3 in pros above), but last time I checked water is much cheaper than FINISH liquid. With Kenmoore, we had to refill the liquid no often than once every 3-4 months. All things considered, we aren't getting anymore BOSCHs. Next time around I am getting a Kenmoore from Sears. They all are regular sizes and don't consume FINISH at lightning speeds.

clean_review_text: [[VIDEOID:491e386908deb8251c574f6eb0c8758f]] There are 12 individually wrapped smal bath bombs in this set. The fragrances are, orange, lavender, frankincense, rosemary, mint, lemongrass, vanilla, seasalt, kiwi, rose, coconut, and papaya. I tried the papaya. The water turned an orange color and that color adhered to my skin and stained my towels but did not stain my foot bath. In summary, these bombs perform terribly, are staining, and are barely fragrant in the bath. The current price of $19.99 is expensive but still competitively priced for this as it comes to $1.67 per bath bomb, which is about what I would expect to pay for bombs of this size. However, at that price, I also expect these to perform and they do not. These are super lackluster and an utter disappointment. I am surprised others rated this 4 or 5 stars. I doubt the other reviewers even tried this in the bath. In the box they look impressive but If they had used at least one, they most likely would not be so impressed.

product_title: Whirlpool 786324 Igniter
clean_review_title: A fraction of the repair cost compared to calling an appliance guy!
clean_review_text: We have an old Caloric brand wall oven that came with the house. I have no idea how old it is, but within a year or two of living here, the oven stopped working. It would glow, but no gas would come out and it would never start. I called an appliance repair man who determined it was a bad ignitor. He explained to me that the gas won't turn on until the ignitor reaches a certain temp, so either the ignitor wasn't getting hot enough, or it wasn't sending the signal to turn on the gas when it did. Either way, the problem was entirely with the ignitor. He replaced it, and something like $200 later, the oven was back to working. Until it stopped working again about a year after that. This time it didn't even glow, the oven would just do nothing. Instead of another expensive repair, I decided on a hunch to assume the part the repair man brought was lower quality than the original. I looked it up online and found a match here on Amazon. I felt confident that I could swap it myself now that I saw the repair guy do it, and sure enough this did the trick for a fraction of what I would pay him to come back! Its been a little over a month since putting it in, and so far so good. I'm hoping this will last longer than the last one, but even if it doesn't, worst case scenario is that I'll spend $20-30 on an annual repair until we can afford to replace the whole oven! I can deal with that.

clean_review_title: Works Well Enough As A Backup, Not Great
clean_review_text: This is a really nice little face washer for a person who needs a very gentle washer, but it doesn't do much exfoliating because the bristles are all very fine and soft. It is exactly as described and pictured and it is working very well so far. With this kind of item, it either matches the photos or it doesn't, and this one looks exactly like the photos, and then it either works or it doesn't, and this one works well enough, but the little balls under the surface don't really seem to do anything other than massage. I suppose they feel nice, but they don't increase the face washing ability of this, so I'm not really sure why they are there. More vibration settings are needed as well as some thicker bristles either on the other side of the unit or on the edge along the top or bottom of the main pad, to make this work better. The listing is accurate and we are satisfied with the performance of this item as a backup unit, but it isn't good enough to be the main one that I use or that anyone else is using. Power consumption is as stated and it is easy to use and easy to work with and we've had no problems with it after quite a while of use. We all basically reached the same conclusion that it needs a better design as to variation of bristles and then more vibration settings. It is very easy to hold, so that was good and that is why it gets four instead of three stars. Many are too hard to hold. This is easy to hold when it is wet and it doesn't become too slippery to keep in my hand. Good luck in your search.

product_title: DROP THE BOMB Bbosong Sun Stick SPF50+ PA++++ 15g - Oil Free UVA/UVB Protection Sebum Control Skin Smooth Moisturizing Sunblock Stick Balm without Sticky, Primer before Makeup
clean_review_title: excellent sunscreen with skin loving ingredients, broad spectrum, great little container
clean_review_text: The "Drop the Bomb" BBOSONG Suns stick uses one of THEE best UVA/UVB sunscreen ingredients on the planet: "Bis-Ethylhexyloxyphenol Methoxyphenyl Triazine"! Say that 5 times fast. Or once. lol. It is a broad spectrum, highly photo stable, chemical sunscreen. Chemical sunscreens mean that they absorb rays and disperse as heat before they can reach skin and do damage. The SPF rating of 50+ and PA++++ means that it offers the highest amount of protection that a sunscreen can offer. Korean skin care, particularly sunscreens, can use super effective ingredients that products made in (or for) USA can not have because the governing bodies have not approved it. It is not because they are unsafe, but because there is a significant amount of bureaucracy involved. No new sunscreen ingredients have been approved in the USA in over 15 years, sad since there are so many wonderful innovations in the rest of the world. We lag greatly behind. Fortunately, we can still buy these products. The other sunscreen used in this product is, Ethylhexyl Methoxycinnamate (Octinoxate)- a less stellar chemical sunscreen that has mixed thoughts on its efficacy, safety, and desirability. It protects only against UVB rays and breaks down when exposed to light (10% per 30-40 minutes). However, when combined with another broad spectrum sunscreen, it compliments it very well. The third sunscreen component is, Octocrylene. Screens out the UVB and somewhat in the UVA II range. It is quite photostable (loses 10% of SPF protection in 95 mins) and is often used to stabilize other photo-unstable UV-filters (octinoxate) It is also often used to improve the water resistance of the products. As for all the other ingredients- primarily stabilizers, viscosity controlling ingredients, hardeners-- basically all ingredients that make it a STICK than can glide onto the skin. It is formulated to melt into the skin on contact, and yet absorb thoroughly. Basically, you won't even know it is there. And like so many other Korean skin care items, there are ingredients that go above and beyond the basic sunscreen-- included are skin loving ingredients (emollients, skin conditioners, soothers) such as Japanese knotweed, water lily, gotu kola (centella asiatica), camellia japonica, Lespedeza Capitata Extract (anti inflammatory), scutellaria (ie scullcap/mint), green tea, licorice, rosemary, chamomile Has a super mild scent- clean, fresh almost like a deodorant, but subtle. Happens to be a in a container that reminds me of deodorant. Super small, cute. Stick in a pocket or purse. I happen to love using it on my hands. I am in my 40s, and while I have been using SPF most of my life on my face, I figured it was high time to remember to put some on my hands. I like keeping this in my purse or my car, and applying it when I go to drive. That seems to be the time that my hands are most consistently exposed to the sun. Figure, hands on the steering wheel and in front of a big window. So yeh, this is perfect for that. I have no problem using this on my face. Doesn't grab skin when used over a moisturizer. If need be can be applied/emulsified in palms then patted on. I prefer it as my "reapply" SPF rather than my first application product (I am used to a cream style). When I do apply it to my face, I always run it over my lips. It is very silky, doesnt have a sunscreen flavor or feel, and it is adding much needed hydration and protection from the sun. Again, I am in my 40s.. clear fair skin but with Rosacea (non acne- just redness and visible veins). Leans dry. Loves Korean skin care. This is so clear, so clean feeling, and so incredibly light that I wish I could show you how it doesn't even feel like anything is on. Not greasy, not even dewy. My skin feels bare. If I have any complaints, it is the use of fragrance. While it smells nice and doesn't really linger.. it would not make a top choice for use on my face. I do however like it for everywhere else and rather enjoy the light scent before it dissipates fully. Even so, it is a really good product, so far. If I experience anything else, I will be back to say so. For all the other ingredients, the each rate 1-2 (very low in terms of hazard to self or environment) on EWG Skin Deep Database.

clean_review_title: Works really well, although don't delude yourself that it won't hurt.
clean_review_text: I'm a tweezing/waxing maniac. Although it does hurt to wax, it doesn't suck as much as having to shave all the time, so I prefer it over shaving. The downside to that is that it is so expensive. In my town, an underarm wax can cost $22 plus tip, and since the hair will grow back at different times you may end up wishing for another wax just a week later. You also have to wait until it's long enough too. Since I was tired of the cost of waxing and I don't mind the pain of waxing or plucking, I thought an epilator would be a good buy. I did a lot of research on each type and finally settled on this one about 6 months ago based on the positive reviews. I made the right choice, for sure, and it has paid for itself at least 5 times over by now. Pros: *It is actually better not to let the hair grow long, so that means you don't have to be hairy for long periods of time *You can do it at any given time, if there is anything to pull, which is way better than a once-per month trip to the waxer's shop. *It doesn't hurt as bad once you are consistent with it, just like with waxing. *It is fairly easy to clean and easy to recharge *The flashlight is a great tool to help see those hairs you would miss just off the ceiling lights. *You can use it almost anywhere, or anywhere you have help from another pair of unwary hands. Bikini line, chin, upper lip, underarm, leg, arm, you name it. My underarms and bikini line are my favorite places to use it. *You can use it in the shower *The battery lasts pretty long, like an hour Cons: *If you try to give yourself a brazilian wax with it by yourself, you are gonna have a bad time. I tried and failed. Luckily, the top part came off before it could do any damage, so it was really just a bit scary. It jammed when that happened, and wouldn't turn back on, but I just plugged it in for a charge and it was back to normal. *It hurts pretty bad. As opposed to waxing, which takes it all off in one go, it pulls hairs one at a time like tweezing, but at a fast pace. That means there is a lot of breath holding and holding of skin to keep it all tight because you don't know when or how many hairs it will find and pull out. I suggest taking 600mg of Ibuprofen a half hour before you intend to do it... or maybe a few glasses of wine if you'd rather not use traditional drugs. *The hair will fly EVERYWHERE, but where else would it go, I guess?

clean_review_text: I bought my LG LTCS20020W top-freezer refrigerator from a local Lowes, so you won't see an Amazon purchase notice. Shrug. I've only owned it for a short while, but here's the skinny: FREEZER: The top freezer hits the ideal 0 degrees F in just a few hours by setting the thermostat on the center position. I deliberately bought a unit with no ice maker on the theory that ice makers are notorious troublemakers. DOWNSIDE: there's NO FREEZER LIGHT. I stuck a little Motion Sensor LED Night Light to the freezer roof to fix the issue. REFRIGERATOR: The refrigerator achieved 37-40 degrees F in a few hours with the thermostat only on the second position. My fridge is a simple unit with LED light, two crisper drawers, a smallish dairy bin and two shelves. Some units include an optional door alarm and pantry drawer, but not mine. OVERALL: This LG top-freezer refrigerator is VERY quiet and energy efficient. The dimensions are excellent for my small kitchen and contains enough food for over a week for my wife and I. This refrigerator is just a tiny bit shorter and narrower than some, which can be a major plus when situating it in some kitchens, especially in older houses. But the refrigerator is an inch or two deeper than some, so measure carefully. I didn't realize it at first, but the doors are reversible. The fridge comes set up with the doors opening to the right, but even an inexperienced person (like myself) can switch the hardware so the doors open to the left in an hour by following the on-line videos. LG refrigerators have a some of the best reputations for reliability, so I'm expecting this LG LTCS20020 to outlast me. Five stars.

Since this offers SPF 50 (P++++), I know my skin is going to be pretty well protected. Since it feels like a moisturizer and goes on clear, I can skip my other skin care if I want. I tend to skip morning skin care in the summer because I am likely to just sweat it off anyway (I have a more involved night time routine to make up for it). So a sunscreen such as this is easy to use, makes my skin feel good, and easy to reapply throughout the day. I dont wear make up usually, but for sake of review I used it under a light foundation tint. Seems to play well- I didnt notice any rolling/pilling. I'll use it up. clean_review_text: I like it. This has probably been the best user reviewed as well as economical model on the market today, but I expected a better result. It runs better then a 1990's machine, but it's not the 4 towel drying monster that I've read... Two large towels will range in time between 1.5hrs to 2 "for me" in the North-East (70 degree day). Also, a regular full load of laundry will take 1.5 hours, where a 1/2 load will take 45mins. Living in 1 of the largest cities in the US (with 1930's wiring) the 110V / 1400W (translates to 12.72 amps) hasn't caused a fire in "my apartment." At the end of the day, I don't think that I can find a better product that will work with my electrical constraints, so although my expectations are tempered... I like this machine. FYI: I need my machine to be very portable, so I put it on a 11.5" x 18" mini-dolly. Although it barely fits, the smooth legs do slip off, so I needed to lock the dryer's mini legs in place. I did this by screwing down round mini tins to the dolly's corners, and wrapped velcro (2 sided, no tape) around the machine and dolly. I used the soft side of the velcro against the machine (prevents scratching), but I also used a little tape-down scratchy pieces on top of the machine pointing up. This way the velcro not-only-sticks-to-itself, but it also doesn't shift aroound on top of the machine.... Finally, I cut a piece of Yoga-mat a little larger then the dolly, and place it under the machine to prevent dryer movement during use...

product_title: Eyebrow Pen, Microblading Eyebrows, 4 Points Eyebrow Pencil, Creates Lasting Make-Up Professional Natural Looking Eyebrows, Cover Sparse Areas, Daily Waterproof Eyebrow Pen with Gift
clean_review_title: Good product but quantity misleading: Only one pen included!
clean_review_text: Is it just me? I have purchased this exact product several times--happily--in dark brown, grey brown, and chestnut (red brown). My eyebrows are, like my natural hair, about a level 4 or 5 dark brown, and the grey brown is probably the closest, though the other colors are handy for changes of color to hair and makeup. My brows are thinning, but a little filling in does the trick, and these pens are my favorite tool for the job, as they both color in where you need solid filling and can be used to make feathery strokes for a more natural brow look. This pen came with a useless little razor and three eyebrow "templates," only one of which I might consider useful (if you are new to shaping your brows). The problem: In the past I have received (I see now from other sellers) at least two or three pens for $6.99 to $8.99. I can't believe I paid over $8 for just one pen from this seller "Generies." Going back over the seller page, I see that indeed, by the pictures it appears that one will receive two pens. A sneaky little trick, showing the same photos as the sellers with better deals, but neglecting to include the "2 CT." If you are busy or distracted in the least, you might fall for it, as did I--and doubtless, as have many customers. I really dislike writing negative comments, but am doing this to save others the trouble and disappointment of feeling ripped off. Needless to say, I will be buying this product from other sellers in future--the ones with the more reasonable--and honest--deals.

product_title: Workman's Friend Ultimate Hand Care Bundle
clean_review_text: This is a very nice hand care bundle for very hands on people. First you get a very nice hand cleaner that contains activated charcoal. This detoxes and cleans very nicely of the toughest dirt, eliminating strong odor but doesn't damage your hands like some strong cleaners might do. Next you get a moisturizing and healing cream. This is great for those who have done some damage to their hands in some physical hand jobs where they hand up with dry and cracked hands. This gives back some moisture and heals those hands. Last but not least is a nice protect and moisturizer. This adds a nice barrier to your hands but also adds much needed hydration to much abused hands that are dry, cracked and scaly. When you have this on your hands it is much easy to wash and wipe away stubborn dirt. This is a nice little set that can also be gifted, especially for people who work with their hands like mechanics or wood workers but many others. This works really well and will help restore some of those much abused hands.

product_title: Philips Norelco G270 Multi-Trim All Purpose Groomer
clean_review_title: It's a groomer, not a haircutter
clean_review_text: The item has the attachments to cut hair but it is not designed for full-on haircuts. It's to keep you groomed between haircuts so you don't have to go as often, hence it being called a groomer. Also, if you're having trouble charging it, that's because you're not likely following what it says right in the instructions...it must be charged for a FULL 24 hours before the first use. Do this, and it works fine. The neckline trimmer is a godsend. My neck hair grows faster than that on my head so this does nicely for a neckshave when I don't really need a haircut yet. Professional haircuts are getting too expensive everywhere to go in every 3-4 weeks when you don't need a full cut but do need a touch-up. This does the trick in that respect. The nose hair attachment works fine...again, the idea is not to get to the point where the hair is so long and thick that it doesn't work. Groom it, keep up with it as it grows. That's what this thing is for. I have misplaced one of the attachments, unfortunately, so will be buying a new one but this does a a great job, better than the bigger, bulkier products out there.

clean_review_text: My mother asked me to find her a moisturizer with matrixxyl and peptides. Very specific and she didn’t want anything that did not have at least those two ingredients. The reason is a conversation with her dermatologist. Matrixxyl is made of 2 peptides, palmitoyl-tripeptide and palmitoyl-oligopeptide, which stimulate collagen and elastin production in the skin. And peptides form a long chain of amino acids to become proteins, penetrating the top layer of skin cells and signaling the cells including collagen. The Cazbe serum works really nicely in tandem with my mom’s Ponds cream (she has used Ponds as long as I remember). The serum absorbs pretty rapidly and unlike other serums, doesn’t seem to leave her skin feeling sort of ‘tight’ or dry. After a few minutes (she will put the serum on before brushing her teeth, and then follow with moisturizer after teeth), she will apply the Ponds cream to completely moisturize her face. I’ve tried this a few times and find I can only really use it around my eyes – it dries out my cheeks and chin. If used just around my eyes, it feels a lot better. Her skin is looking beautiful. I think in combination with a really solid cream to sort of ‘seal in’ the benefits, after washing your face, and just before bed, it’s a great part of your beauty routine. * we received the serum for evaluation and review

clean_review_text: A good product with proven calming ingredients. These ingredients overlap with some sleeping pills, and this product can make you feel tired. For many people, these pills are best for after the work day is over and closer to bed time. If you are hoping for a flow state at work or an alternative to ADHD drugs, this product is not intended for that. If that is your goal, start with 200-400mg of L-theanine by itself to see how far that gets you. L-Theanine is an inexpensive pleasant tasting powder and it will be calming without making you tired. The price, as I write this, is $1/serving. That price is reasonable if you consider the premium ingredients, like KSM-66 Ashwagandha. You are paying for convenience by having a company blend powders and put them in a capsule. People in the target market can afford $1/serving. The product is made in the USA at a GMP facility.

product_title: Abitzon 2019 Non Toxic Nail Polish Set Eco Friendly Easy Peel Off & Quick Dry Water Based Nail Polish (10 Bottles)
clean_review_text: Ok...I'm going to try to not write a novel here, but I am sooo in love with these polishes!!! I read a ton on reviews on these peel off polishes and finally chose these not expecting a whole lot. I have 100% no complaints!!! But I will say that I guess it all depends on what you are wanting out of your nail polish. If you are wanting it to last like it lasts on artificial nails... this is not for you. If you are wanting options and to not have to deal with nail polish remover...THIS IS FOR YOU!!! I have loved painting my nails and having polished nails since a kid. I've always hated nail polish remover. I have thin nails and can't change my polish often because nail polish remover dries my nails out and makes them even weaker and it stinks...even nail polish stinks for that matter. To make the polish last I've always added a top coat about every day or two and I've learned how to do touch ups that hardly show when the polish on tip of the nail (natural nails) chipped or wore away, so that I didn't have to use acetone any more often than necessary. But it was really self defeating because the more polish I had on, the more acetone I had to use to remove it. So anyway...I got these little gems in...they are small....but then again, when you compare the price to buying regular polish ( I buy OPI normally ) and the fact that you are getting several colors of these for the price you'd pay for 1 or 2 bottles of decent regular polish, it's not a bad deal at all. I tried them right away and #1...they are hardly smell at all! I don't smell them unless I actually hold my nails or the bottle to my nose to smell them. #2...the polish went on fine. It's a little thick, but it settles down smooth as it dries. #3 I love some of the colors and some will be just for having fun, but they are fun t play with. 5 colors are opaque and 4 are transparent, so they are fun mixing and matching. I like using the transparent ones as top coats on the opaque ones. #4 They lasted about 3 days with no chips and very very little edge wear (with the daily addition of a top coat for shine) until I couldn't stand it any longer and had to try to peel them off. Some of the reviews said they were hard to peel off, but I had no problem at all. And all but one came off in one full piece! Amazing!!! So easy and NO ACETONE!!! And the other thing I like is that with regular polish, I would sometimes try and peel of as much as I could before using the acetone and some would come off but my nail always seemed super dry under the polish even when it was peeled off. That is not the case with these at all. My nails were not dry at all. They were still healthy and not dry at all!!! Oh and the BONUS ....since they peel off, any polish that gets on to your skin peels off easy too after it dries. :)

clean_review_title: Non-Fluoride Toothpaste with Ginger Root Extract and Aloe Vera
clean_review_text: According to the manufacturer, "Fresh Aid Toothpaste is packed with quality ingredients that target gum diseases like gingivitis and plaque. It prevents tooth decay and gum and tooth pain. It's also the best toothpaste for those with sensitive teeth and gums." This toothpaste has ginger root extract, which I can taste and may partially account for the tingling sensation that stays on after you rinse your mouth out. The toothpaste has a yellowish color and kind of reminds me of lemon filling. It leaves your mouth feeling fairly clean but there is a lightness to this toothpaste that other white and chalky toothpastes (like Crest) don't have. I question whether the lack of fluoride will be effective in the long-term at protecting my recessed gums and preventing plaque from taking hold. I usually use Colgate Total, so only time will tell if this toothpaste will be as effective. I have been brushing it now for only a week, so I will update this review if anything changes. For now, it's an OK toothpaste for those who are concerned with the amount of chemicals in their toothpaste. Because of my gum issues, effectiveness is more of a priority for me, so if you have overall healthy teeth and gums, you may love it.

clean_review_text: I was looking for a sleeping mask that I could use on an upcoming international trip. I have trouble sleeping when it is not totally dark, so sleeping when travelling in a plane, train or other mode of transportation has always been difficult. I wanted to find a sleeping mask that was both comfortable to wear and blocked out all light. I ordered the Seedling's 3D Contoured Cup Sleeping Mask and it has really hit the mark on what I was looking for. Good: 1. Comfortable to wear. It is not flat. Cushion runs around the outer edge of the mask so it does not touch any part of your eyes. 2. Blocking light. I tested wearing the mask during the daytime and to my surprise there was no light seepage. It was totally dark. Since the mask has contoured cups around your eyes you can open them while wearing to confirm no light was coming in. 3. Material. The mask feels like it made out of some neoprene/spandex material. It is smooth against the skin. 4. Bonus. It comes with a bonus silk mask and ear plugs. Bad: 1. Nothing to note. Although one suggestion I would make is to include a carrying case for the mask instead of the bonus silk mask. Since I plan to use the mask when I travel, I have to figure out how to protect it in my baggage when not being used. Overall I would highly recommend this sleeping mask.

I like this drip method very much and don't miss the daily hassle of cleaning out the coffee press. Tastes great. I am going to buy one to travel with. I like the red color it is easy to spot! All the parts fit my dryer and I liked that the kit included all the parts that I would need to replace. clean_review_text: I really like this shampoo by Giovanni. I was not sure what to expect to be honest, but I was pleasantly surprised. clean_review_text: [[VIDEOID:deb7a7b9f478e538d957cb4003f30987]] This is the egg rack that I've been looking for! It stacks vertically so it minimizes its footprint in the fridge, it holds three layers of 10 eggs each. The super cool feature I was not expecting is the shelves that hold the eggs are spring loaded and automatically fold up out of the way when that shelf is empty. The weight of a single egg is enough to keep the shelf deployed downward, as soon as the last egg is lifted it automatically folds up and out of the way to expose the next layer of eggs. It seems to be made slightly too thin plastic, I don't think it's going to last , long. Another issue is mine received a very small crack during shipping, which is not the end of the world except it is in a very vulnerable location, in the very center of the top rack., So structurally it is in the weakest part of the shelf. That's a bad spot because it could easily spread. Perhaps if they made this out of slightly heavier plastic this would not be an issue. Another flaw is that if you stack two eggs directly on top of each other they can touch, this isn't a real big problem just something I noticed.

product_title: SockStar Sock Clips - Gold Edition Tin Gift Box (20 Clips)
clean_review_text: I have used several types of sock clips for my work (thin cotton) socks. These are good clips and I would give a 5-star rating except they are difficult to get my socks clipped into the device. It takes me longer than with other clips. I have had no problems with sock becoming loose in the wash or dryer, and this is with quite thin socks. DETAILS: My favorite clip is [[ASIN:B003E6DK4U SockPro Sock Holders Red]]. You can get them in various colors and they are simple to put your socks into them and I have had very minimal loose socks after many (~15) washes with each clip. You just pull the ends through the socks and it is almost a one hand operation once you get used to it. I have not liked [[ASIN:B00I001GKU 10 Pcs Mix Color Socks Stockings Hose Clip Holder Organizer Rack Laundry Supplies]] as well. They grip the socks much looser than the ones I recommend and I am loosing socks 15-20% of the time. I have also used [[ASIN:B000QOHCFQ 24 SockLocks, Colors May Vary]] with good success. They are a harder plastic where the ones I recommend are softer and more flexible. I thought that the SockLocks would not hold up well to the hot wash and may become brittle. They seem quite flimsy; however they have worked well through at least 10 wash/dry cycles and given the price they may be a better purchase than the SockPro item. These SockStar clips are even tighter holding than the other clips I have used. They are thicker plastic and seem well made. I like the color on them and think they will hold up well. I have only used a few clips through 2 wash/dry cycles, so I do not know the longer term reliability, but I expect that it will be fine. If you have had problems with other sock clips loosing grip in the wash, then I would suggest these. They take me a bit longer to get my socks clipped is the only negative I have. I may be a little stingy in taking off a star...

product_title: Allegra K Women Long Sleeves Scoop Neck Mesh Panel Top L Black
clean_review_title: Comparison of L vs. XL top - for those who wear a 12, 14 or 16 top!
clean_review_text: Having received conflicting advice/opinions on whether size L or XL would fit me best, I opted to purchase both sizes, as I am a Prime member, and could purchase this top for less than $13 with free returns. For reference, I am 5' 4 1/2" tall, with a 38DD/40D bust (depends on brand), a 35" waist and 45" hips. In tops, I typically wear a regular 14, 12W or L top, although depending on clothing brand, may wear an XL, sometimes even a 14W. On occasion, I find that a regular 12 or even a M will fit me best. Regular size 16 is typically too large for me, and I look like I'm swimming in 16W or 1X tops. My upper arms are on the heavier size, so if a top has tight sleeves, it can present an issue for me. I have an hourglass figure; neither top- or bottom-heavy, proportionate with a defined waist. I like my clothing to be figure-flattering and to enhance my shape, with a classic, tailored fit, not loose/baggy but NEVER tight, pulling or overly clingy. I don't mind pieces that are cut close to the body, but anything that shows lumps or creates a muffin top is out - I hate looking as though I have squeezed myself into a too-small size for vanity's sake. I like my sleeve length slightly on the longer side, about 21" from the shoulder seam, so that the sleeve hits above the wrist bone just at the point where my wrist and hand join (this sleeve length covers my watch when I am wearing one). So how did this top fit? I was surprised to find that BOTH the L and XL tops worked for me! The mesh portion of the top fits almost identically in both sizes - it's generously cut and offers a lot of stretch so that my upper arms did not look sausage-like in either size. I did not find that the sleeves were freakishly long, as some reviewers have commented; while long, they were only an inch or so longer than my preferred sleeve length, so I am not likely to shorten them (I often wear my long sleeved shirts with the sleeves pushed up to about 3/4 length). For both tops, the sleeve length was about the same, only a tiny bit longer in the XL than the L. The real difference between the sizes was in the jersey body of the top. On me, the XL had a slightly - and only slightly - looser fit than shown in the images worn by the model, still shaped, slim fitting and flattering. The L was much more figure-hugging, cut very close to the body; not as close as a leotard, and not tight or pulling, but snug. Both fit me - I agree with reviewers who've stated that the cut of this top is more along the line of juniors' sizing than misses - the torso is narrower than I find with misses sizing. On neither size (both were tried on with and without a bra) did I find the issue with the inside lining across the bust that others have mentioned - despite being a 38DD/40D, I did not have any noticeable line across my bust with this top. It does appear that the extra lining can easily be cut out if needed, as another reviewer has mentioned. The verdict? For my current size, I will most likely opt to wear the XL, as I don't plan to wear this top with a jacket. Perfect for a night out with the girls or date night with my husband, it's cute and stylish, comfortable, I like the shaped fit, and the shoulder seams are where they should be - not halfway down my upper arms, as I find with some other XL tops. That said, I am in the midst of a significant weight loss effort - I've lost more than 50 pounds over the past 14 months. I would expect that by January/February 2016 as my efforts continue (given that we've just entered the holiday eating season!) the L will prove to be a better fit. So I'll keep both! My advice: - If you are a size 16 regular or 14W, choose the XL top. The L will be much too tight across your torso. - If you are a size 14 regular or 12W, choose the XL top if you want a fit slightly looser through the torso (but still shaped) than shown on the model, if you like a snug, body-hugging fit through the torso, choose the L top. - If you are a size 12 regular, choose the L top; you may even want to opt for the M (although this is not one that I ordered). - In general, if you typically purchase junior sizes and are narrower in the torso, go with the L; if you prefer a misses cut and are a bit wider through the torso, choose the XL. - And if you're a Prime member, buy them both with the free returns option and decide for yourself! Hope that this was helpful!

clean_review_text: [[ASIN:B01ETYVW3G Love For Women, 1.9 Fl. Oz. / 55 ml - Inspired By Onika By Nicki Minaj Perfume]] I really enjoy trying different parfums to help me find a new fresh scent or to combine with other scents to come up with my own combined signature scent. The seller does a wonderful job of delivering quickly and well packaged. The cologne is very pretty in it's design in a feminine curved bottle with black lace on the front which looks nice on my dresser or vanity.This perfume definitely smells great. It has a sheer musk undertone that is very inviting, you can also smell fruitiness and a slight hint floral and sweet undertones like flowers. It is a very feminine scent but also very seductive but not overpowering. It's difficult to describe scents but all I can say is it really does smell wonderful, it doesn't give me a headache and my fiance loves it. I find that it lasts longer if I spay it on pulse points. I receive complements a lot of times while wearing it I definitely recommend this wonderful perfume and would absolutely say try it. This is a 1.9 ounce bottle at under 13.00 delivered to your door. I purchased this product at a discounted price in exchange for my honest review. The discount I received will not alter my honest opinion in any way. Since I count on honest product reviews to make my selections on Amazon, I will always be forthcoming and sincere in all my reviews. No matter if the product is discounted or not, I will leave honest feedback that I base on my own personal experience with the product I receive. *One last note, I wasn't sure what the difference in Eau De Parfum and Perfume is, so I looked it up. Here's what I found on the web on perfume website: Eau de parfum. "After the top notes have died away, the middle notes or heart notes of a perfume become noticeable. This is the focus of eau de parfum which makes it perfect to spray on hair or clothing. (Be careful with delicate fabrics such as silk, though, as they may stain.) The concentration of oils is over 15%, sometimes reaching as high as 20%. Since it is less intense than perfume extract, it is also cheaper, but it usually lasts well and is sold in small sizes." So it is stronger than Eau de Cologne which contains about 7% essence dissolved in alcohol of 60º or 70º, and stronger than Eau de toilette which contains around 10% aromatic essence. But not as strong as Perfume. This is the strongest of scents because of the higher concentration of essences – as much as 40% by volume.

I am not blaming this particular brand, of course, but I think that people should only buy the half-moon if there is someway of supporting the half umbrella higher up, such as a Velcro strap ( not an option in my case given how the balcony is arranged ). When it's just a normally windy day, this stand is stable enough though; I just feel like a full circle case would impart a bit more stability on the extremely gusty days.

My hair is straight & long (nearly to my butt) so keeping it free of tangles is pretty important. This little comb is slightly smaller than a credit card & can fit anywhere a CC can. This means I can even toss it in my cash box, leave it on my vendor table, put it in my pocket, wallet, purse, side purse pocket, slide it in with my camera case or iPod case, Kindle case... you get the idea. product_title: ESPRO Toroid Milk Frothing Pitcher - Stainless Steel Milk Steamer Pitcher for Milk Frothing and Steaming, Espresso Accessories for Coffee Bar, 12 Ounce
clean_review_text: Pros: The pitcher is nicely shaped and does work for steaming as described-- by pointing the steam wand at the bottom of the pitcher. Cons: Kinda pricey understates it, no? It's really a shortcut that, in the end, though stylish and handsome, is not necessary. Practice makes perfect. Items like this and steaming thermometers are aimed at new recruits in the coffee army who become frustrated during boot camp and finally want something that just works. Believe me, I understand, but immerse yourself in videos, online advice, and visit good coffee shops (no, not *$s!). Watch the baristas. Some shops offer classes. While it might seem silly to take a class in "milk chemistry" or such, it's not. You'll gain by watching, listening, and speaking with others trying to master the craft. If you love the look of this pitcher, and I don't blame you if you do-- it's cool looking, for sure-- buy it. But if it's just that you're not having luck steaming milk with a new machine, keep working. Avoid gimmicks that promise you a no effort shortcut. In the end, you'll be a better barista if you just stick with it, get the advice and instruction you need, and finally (and you will) master producing creamy microfoam. Cheers, mates.

product_title: Professional Facial Steamer: Face Steamer for Facial Deep Cleaning with Aromatherapy Kit and Blackhead Removal Tools, Vaporizador Facial Steamer for face. (Pink)
clean_review_title: A Cosmetologist's view: Easy to put together, easy-to-use, and feels great.
clean_review_text: This facial steamer comes in pieces in a plain cardboard box. There are instructions on how to put it together and how to use it, but it's pretty self-explanatory. Once together, you fill the steamer cup to the max line with hot tap water, add essential oils to a cotton swab and stick it in the aromatherapy bud holder next to the nozzle, if desired, and turn the heat and light on. Within just a few moments, there's a steady stream of steam coming out of the nozzle. It goes a fair distance, but to get the best coverage of your face with the steam, you will want to be about 15 inches below the nozzle. The steam feels really wonderful and relaxing! It runs for about 4 to 5 minutes and really opens your pores to help clean away any impurities. I really like the simplicity of this facial steamer, but would not consider it professional quality. The mechanical portion and cup are made of very lightweight plastic, do not seem extremely durable, and it is very basic in its function. I have been a cosmetologist for over 30 years, and this is more suited for home use. Using this facial steamer is a great way to take care of your skin and a wonderful way to spend time doing some self-care. There's nothing like spending some time a day or two each week and really taking good care of your skin. This facial steamer will help you do that in the comfort of your own home, and save you money at the same time. I'm really enjoying it and would definitely recommend it!

product_title: Crystala Filters LT700P Refrigerator Water Filter Replacement LG LT700P, Kenmore 469690, ADQ36006101,9690, ADQ36006102, LFX31925ST, LFX31945ST (3 Pack)
clean_review_text: This fits in my LG LFXS30766S refrigerator, which uses LG LT700P filters. This is NSF 42 certified, so it is not a premium filter. If you want one that adds NSF 372 certification, AQUACREST ADQ36006101 is the one I replaced to install this new one. Waterdop also offers a premium line of filters. You have to flush these with water on the first use. The first couple glass fills I discarded. After that I flushed this by putting the water into my Pur room-temp water filter. It took a minute of flushing for the cloudy water to go away. You can put the date sticker on the outside cover, instead of on the cartridge. I write the exact install date on the cartridge using a permanent marker. Six months is just an estimate. I use mine for longer. If you have a larger household you might go through these sooner. I usually buy filters in multi-packs of filters to save money. They do not go bad. The only thing inside is carbon. Nearly all the filters come from China these days.

I wash and condition every day using hard water and use at least three styling products daily. I’m scheduled to get my hair colored in five days and figured my hair looks lousy because my hair needs coloring and perhaps because I’ve tried a number of different shampoos lately instead of sticking with my usual products. As it turns out, however, I simply needed a good clarifying shampoo. A clarifying shampoo is used to get rid of build up from hair products, minerals from hard water, chlorine, pool chemicals, oils, etc. This Organic Doctor charcoal clarifying shampoo is black, thick and has a strong scent. This shampoo has an emphasis on organic and natural ingredients and looks better than a lot of shampoos as it doesn’t have many common chemicals (e.g., SLS, phalates and parabens), but it does appear to still contain some chemicals such as fragrance. The scent smells heavy and masculine to me and I’m not crazy about it, but surprisingly my hair smells good after it’s rinsed and dried. And the strong scent doesn’t trigger my allergies or cause any irritation. The first application didn’t lather much, but the second one created generous lather that I massaged through my hair and rinsed easily. My hair looks restored as it’s shiny and manageable again. It feels and looks healthy, and my color looks good. I had no idea my issues were due to the build up of things like minerals and hair care products. I’ll gladly endure the smell and use this once or twice a month to clarify (more often may affect my color) and swimmers and some others may benefit from using this more often. 4 stars.

product_title: Bold Men, Mens Hair Gel"Casual Hold" 100ml, Mens Styling Gel
clean_review_text: So, for a man who thinks his looks strike a resemblance to that of Anderson Cooper.. (bc, of the devilishly handsome silver fox short crew cut) mixed with a longer (3/4”) faux-hawk frontal vibe.. this is a great product to work with this style as it complements the cut really well! As one who’s always used hair gel (daily x30+ yrs!) it’s hard to find one we have not tried. Bold Men casual- sold just under $20 for 3.3oz. Is a higher price point so, the expectations were high and happy to say, this came through impressively. This gel has an ability to last thru the day without becoming razor sharp quills nor, a crusty-crumbly flake after a full day of wear in tear. Being one who prefers a touchable hold (the hardest combo to find btw!!) this gives that to you.. We found, unlike cheaper store bought brands- less is more! (too much will weigh down the hair) **A trick of the trade for any who want to accomplish short spikes- use less product!! Best results came from using no more than a nickel size amount onto towel (50%) dried hair.. and finger combing into place. The hold stays movably put.. performing better then ever expected- yay!! TBH, this may take getting used too.. IF, you’re norm is the “frozen gel look!” Hack: one can finger style hair into place with this- then, use blow dryer on a lower setting for just a moment.. it will expedite the setting process- giving specific direction to the final look. (A convertible would do the same but.. not sure It’s feasible in winter ha ha!) Product is thick yet, offers just enough slip to give you pliability to style before it settles. Note: color is clear… It’s just the container that is black! After hair is set, this absorbs vs. shellacs to the hair- letting hair have movement and touchable texture.. The scent is nice with a masculine appeal And after 3wks. of daily application we’ve used approx. a tad over 1/4 of content (to give you an idea how long it will last..) thus far, washing daily we’ve noticed no unwanted build up.. which is also a big deal- as this can lead to flakes/Dandruff..!! A good shampoo scrub removes product easily enough.. and paired with daily conditioner, it’s not over-drying to the hair. Making the $ seem worth all the quality benefits. This is a top quality higher end product that best fits those who have a moveable set style they like their hair to maintain throughout the day.. this can achieve the desired look and look fantastic- and does so, without all the unwanted side effects that can come with daily use of hair gel.. I hope this info can help your decision… Feel free to let me know!

I wish there was a way to have it rotate through all the songs instead of play "Twinkle Twinkle Little Star" 500 times in a row, but other than that there's really nothing about this that I don't like. The light is soft and you can select the color of light you prefer or have it fade through the whole rainbow. A rechargeable battery would really knock this out of the park so I could have the option to use it without being plugged in, but it's not something I really need I guess. I would definitely recommend this to any new parent!

clean_review_title: Simple Boxes But Look Special In Gold▪️Hold Up Fine For Lightweight Gifts▪️Store Flat & EZ Assembly
clean_review_text: These PackQueen 6”x6”x4” square champagne gold boxes are attractive all occasion boxes. These are practical as they ship and store flat and are quick and easy to assemble. These aren’t made of heavy duty cardboard, but the materials are more than adequate for lighter weight gifts and they make a good impression in the gold color with a crisp white interior. They hold their form best when I take a moment to bend the creases before assembly. The bottoms are formed by interlocking flaps that fit together well and lay fairly flat, but these aren’t heavy duty, strong boxes. While not necessarily needed, I do like to add a little tape inside to reinforce the bottoms. It doesn’t show, and the bottoms feels more sturdy. But I have used a couple without tape, and they haven’t collapsed as they feel more secure once I add tissue or filling with a gift and tie a ribbon around the box or wrap it. These boxes are perfect for lightweight items like cosmetics, candles, a special ornament, small clothing items such as slipper socks, lingerie or a tee, and small accessories like gloves or a scarf. These are practical and pretty when finished off with a nice ribbon or bow. I find it convenient to already have these on hand as the need for a box arises, and the champagne gold color looks elegant. These make a good impression and exceed my expectations at the current price of $18.99 for 30 boxes ($.64 a box). Recommended. 5 stars.

product_title: Sleep Headphone Stereo Headphone + Soft Fleece Earmuffs Most Comfortable Headphones for Sleeping - Perfect for Air Traveling Accessories
clean_review_text: After buying Sleep Phones for several years (6 pairs of them, I believe) and having each pair break internally by the plug, I decided it was time to try something less expensive. That way, even if the cord breaks, I won't have spent as much money. So far, that's working out fine. Here's my comparison of Afunta Sleep Headphones with the Sleep Phones. I have had the Afunta Sleep Headphones now for a while, and here's what I've noticed. The speakers are hard, thicker than the Sleep Phones speakers and aren't flexible. On the other hand, they're comfortable if you lie on your back, and I find them perfectly comfortable when the side of my head is on my pillow unless I'm using a harder pillow, which I don't recommend you do. These headphones are quieter than the Sleep Phones, which are quieter than my regular headphones, but are loud enough to listen to audiobooks or music to fall asleep to. They're usually loud enough to wear doing chores, although they may not be loud enough to do dishes with. I haven't had the Afuntas long enough for the speaker wires to break, but I never heard back from Sleep Phones when I emailed them about the broken ones, and besides - they don't pay shipping for replacement parts, and after owning so many pairs that broke in the same spot, this would add up to the cost of at least another pair of Sleep Phones. One odd things others have mentioned about the Afuntas is that the speakers are loose in the headband. I adjusted to that pretty quickly, but if you throw your headphones down on the night table like I do, expect the speakers to be located in a different place in the band than they were when you removed them. The bottom line for me is this: the Afunta Sleep Headphones are comfortable for me, I can listen to my audio books and do chores and workouts while wearing them and they cost about half of what the Acoustic Sheep Sleep Phones cost. If I slept with one of my ears on a firm pillow, these might not be the best for me - the Sleep Phones would be - but since I sleep on soft pillows, I'm happy with my Afuntas and their lower price.

clean_review_title: Blocks air flow more than it should
clean_review_text: This is unlike the actual LG LT120F filter. The LG filter is a solid carbon block with holes allowing airflow. This product is a mesh with a fabric backing. Proper airflow is essential to the functioning of a refrigerator as air circulates from the freezer to the refrigerator and back to the freezer. The circulation fan is not powerful, and I am concerned that this 3rd-party filter will block the airflow. Air has to somehow make it through this fabric. The fabric is glued to the plastic and cannot easily be removed. LG refrigerators have enough problems already. The goal of this filter is to remove odors. Carbon does that. It is not designed to clean dust out of the air. So having a fabric filter is inappropriate for this product. Other third-party filters look like the LG one, where you can see through the holes to the other side.

product_title: Frigidaire EFIC452-SSRED XL Maker, Makes 40 Lbs. of Clear Square Ice Cubes A Day, Stainless, Red Steel
clean_review_text: It took a bit of trial and error, but, I got the machine working well. Instructions could have been a lot clearer. Water is poured into a reservoir that holds about 1 1/2 liters. The machine produces a "rack" of ice about every 15 minutes. Four cycles will fill the catch box holding the ice. This needs to be emptied after 20 minutes or so since the ice will begin to melt as if it were in a refrigerator. The melt drains off and recycles through the machine so it is not a problem if one doesn't mind smaller ice cubes; they are small to begin with. The machine works by water flowing from a horizontal tube with many very small holes that allow water to flow over the ice-making rack. It is obvious that the water put into the reservoir must be scrupulously clean. I currently use only filtered water. I think this is essential to prevent a breakdown. The size of the cubes does vary and can be adjusted. Some people put the fresh ice into bags before putting the ice in a freezer. This is helpful since the fresh ice will "clot" and need to be separated somewhat. I use a wood mallet for the purpose. If the ice is placed into a plastic bag just dropping it on a counter should work to separate the ice. The making of ice is quite noisy. If I am in the same room as the machine, it sounds like a truck going by. I am impressed so far. Fridgedaire put a lot of thought into the machine. Cheaper versions of ice-makers have a high rate of negative reviews while Fridigaire has good reviews overall. You will pay for that— It's worth it.

clean_review_title: Made in China. It's nice as long as you have the right expectations.
clean_review_text: I’ve used a couple of these icemakers. There are quite a few of them being sold by different Chinese companies on Amazon right now. They may look slightly different, but they all operate the same. As long as you have proper expectations, they work fairly well. The listing shows that this icemaker can make 27 pounds of ice per day. That would only be the case if someone empties the ice basket each and every time that it is full. It takes about an hour for it to feel, so you would have to have someone to constantly empty it, which is probably not going to happen. This icemaker makes 9 cubes of ice at a time. They then empty into the holding basket which is on top of the water reservoir. Once the basket is full, it will stop making ice. If you do not remove the ice than the eye simply melts back down into the water reservoir, and the process starts all over again. You can remove the ice cubes and place them in a freezer, but the ice cubes are wet so when you place the cubes into the freezer they will freeze together in a clump. So you have to use an ice pick or something else to break them apart. It’s best to turn this icemaker off before you go to bed, or when you are leaving the office, and not let it run when no one is there to use or remove the ice. Otherwise, as stated above, the eye simply melts in the process of refreezing continues again and again. This icemaker is made in China and being sold by Chinese company on Amazon.

product_title: Keurig My K-Cup Universal Reusable K-Cup Pod Coffee Filter, Compatible with Keurig Classic/1.0 K-Cup Pod Coffee Makers, 1 Count, White
clean_review_text: I ordered two of these to replace a couple of older ones because the lids cracked in half (see photo). I've had four or five of these lids break along the left side, up to the red disc. Happens every time after a few months of use. Anyway, I haven't had the new ones long enough to see if they too will break, but I'm not a fan of the redesign. The new design has a wire basket with a lid, like the old one, but the basket then goes inside a plastic shell with a single hole in the bottom. So preparing one for use requires two steps instead of one, and cleaning is a pain. The old basket had a "handle" that I used to rap on the edge of a bowl to shake the grounds loose (I save my grounds for gardening). The new basket lacks the handle and is generally sort of flimsy, making it difficult to empty. Also, the new design allows you to make either a cup or a carafe. Okay, not a bad idea, except that now you have to go through another menu, in which you must select either a picture of a carafe or a number, 1-6; then you get to another screen where you can select the size. There's really no explanation of what the numbers mean (I know, it's probably in the Keurig manual somewhere) so we just guess and then see what we get. The whole thing is annoying. Amazon is famous for reducing transactional friction; it's disappointing when a company goes out of its way to increase friction. On the plus side, a cup of coffee seems to brew more quickly. Haven't actually timed the two models, but it feels faster. Regarding Keurig customer service: I recently posted a question about the cracking lids, and someone from Keurig suggested that I call customer support. I did, and it was a waste of time. The rep took all my personal info down and said he would pass it on to someone else who might be able to do something about replacing the broken lids. Or not, he wasn't sure. That was the last I've heard from them.

clean_review_text: I was skeptical because I don't like to buy things made of synthetic materials, but I got a Purple Royal and I'm trying it out. Here's what I like about it so far: I sit on a futon sofa much of the day with my laptop, and the sofa is depressed where I've been sitting, so I'm sinking into the futon. The Purple Royal is 2 inches thick and lifts me up, and out of the sinkhole, distributing weight well across the futon mattress. It feels cool to the backside, and allows plenty of spread instead of being flattened and squinched. I'm using it with the black cover, but I'm going to put a pillowcase over it to match the cover on the futon mattress. Another thing I like is that I can tuck it right at the back of the sofa and it does not come all the way out to the front of the sofa; there's about 4-5" space between it and the edge of the sofa, which allows room for my legs to rest on the edge of the sofa instead of raising them up and cutting off blood flow. I thought about getting the 2.25" one ("double") but that one has a "soft side" and I can't see any need for anything softer. This one feels plenty soft and roomy. I think the "simply purple" one (1.5") wouldn't cushion enough. I will update if I notice anything different.

clean_review_title: Powerful, looks great, easy to clean
clean_review_text: This range is great. It's super powerful, I used the center burner for the first time the other day and it cooked things in the wok perfectly (and fast!). I like that there are different sized burners. I usually cook for one, and a small pot will boil over on simmer on all but the smallest burner. Just move it to that one if you're having issues with it being too hot. It looks great, the grates are nice quality and flat, plus they sort of lock onto the bottom so they're all lined up nicely. I like that underneath the burners is all one surface, so you're not scooping out around each burner all the time to clean it. Simply take the grates off and you can wipe the entire bottom down if you want to, including swiping pieces off the side to be able to get them out. There are 2 annoying things. First that the ignition continues for a long time after the flame is on, it takes some getting used to to operate the knobs..also that it won't go past high if you're still pushing on the knob. I'm used to it after a few weeks now though. The other thing is the surface the knobs are on looks dirty if you wipe it with a sponge, you have to get a cloth or paper towel to clean it. I didn't realize that the knobs had so many indentations either, those are not the easiest to clean. I had it professionally installed, so I don't know how easy it was.

Needless to say, that was very annoying, considering the point of using the cream was to try to eliminate the read sores. This cream has a nice, thick consistency and spreads far more easily than the other two brands I've mentioned. The big advantage to buying this in bulk on Amazon is that it no costs me no more than the tubes of Desitin that I used to buy. I've already used many other Method products for the home (French lavender all-purpose cleanser, hand soap, etc.), and this product has only strengthened my love for the Method brand. I'd definitely recommend it to all parents.

clean_review_text: So far this is not working very well for me. The wrap itself appears well made, is easy to put on and comfortable. The instructions are awful and it takes a bit of time to figure out how to use it. Apparently the manufacturer/distributor is aware of this and sent me an email informing me if it didn't work they would replace it. So the question is what is it supposed to do. Using the "red" setting for intensity and time and waiting for 15 minutes to warm up I could barely feel any heat, only slight warmth (not very therapeutic) over the course of 30 minutes or so. Maybe for the price that's about all one should expect. I'll be shopping for a better one. 4/1/18 Update. After periodic use of the wrap I am changing my rating from two stars to four stars because after use I discovered the heat is adequate, although it could be a little hotter. The major problem, as noted by others, is the control. It remains unclear how to keep the wrap in the warmest (Red) mode, and as another reviewer noted you have to keep checking to make sure it hasn't switched to cooler (White or Blue) modes. I suspect there may be a way to eliminate this problem but with such poor documentation it is impossible so far to keep it working correctly. In summary, this is a nice well made wrap that attaches to the knee well. If the cord was longer and the controls worked better this could be a very good wrap for applying heat to the knees. 4/8/18 Update. After continued use I am changing my rating from 4 to 5 stars. I have found that when tightly wrapped (which is easy to do because of the softness, flexibility and large velcro attachments) using the red setting (hottest) it heats to a very comfortable temperature. The setting controls could be improved or maybe better instructions should have been provided. This is what I am now doing. After pressing the large plastic square control button for a second or two, color/temp is selected. I press it when it lights red (this is the hottest setting; it also has two lower ones-white and blue). After a few minutes the setting changes to a cooler (white or blue) color. I then re-press the red and it stays at this setting/temp, until I turn it off. It really works well, provides a very comfortable warm, pleasant temp and fits very well over the knee.area.

clean_review_text: Anyone knows-use acetone to remove the toughest of nail polishes-or so I thought! This stuff works! Literally works! My youngest loves to get her nails done all the time, whether it be at home done by her or myself, but mostly going to a salon. Just last week she went with her friend and got her nails done in cute but a little too light pink gel. The first couple of days she was ok with it, but then it grew old quickly. I told her to try this new remover I got. She opened it and when it didn't have the strong smell that acetone has, she said MOM I think you got the wrong stuff-no way will this take off gel polish. But we followed the directions using a cotton ball saturated and within 4 minutes, her polish was coming off easily. A little more applied and it was gone! Poof-just like that and the "oil" actually made her nails shiny and not slimy or oily and her cuticles looked like she had them soaked and cuticle cream applied. They literally felt softer-much, much better than when you use acetone! The oils in the remover are plant based and soak into the cuticle bed just as if you moisturized them. Great stuff! I was offered this at a discounted price for my review of the product. I am glad I got this-for under $13 you will get a big glass bottle of the remover and it should last you a very long time. Yes you do want to make sure to saturate the cotton ball you use or just apply directly but because of the consistency, a little goes a long way!

product_title: Ultra Durable 154701001 Dishwasher Splash Shield Replacement Part by BlueStars - Exact Fit for Frigidaire & Kenmore Dishwashers - Replaces 1465007 154685101 AP4338941 PS2203346 - Pack of 2
clean_review_text: These splash shields look and fit exactly like the factory originals. The only problem I've had is that they seem to be stiffer than the original version. This is an issue because of the flawed design of the bottom rack in the Whirlpool/Maytag/etc. dishwasher where the rollers press down on the splash shields when the rack is rolled in and out to load/unload. This pressure seems to be why these splash shields fail so often. Since these don't flex as well as the originals, they are prone to be dislodged when the rack rolls over them. This can cause leakage. It can also cause the shields to fail even sooner since they don't flex as well. As a matter of interest, I recently checked the current models of the Whirlpool, etc. dishwashers and they don't use this splash shield at all but have redesigned the door and the door seal. I guess the engineers realized this was a constantly failing part.

clean_review_text: Eeeeeeekkk I'm so excited about this!! I love my Dry Bar Buttercup hair dryer, but really wanted a diffuser for it because I like to scrunch my hair time to time. When I added it to my cart, it was $21 I believe. That's still cheaper than Sephora so I was going to get it but waited. SO GLAD I DID. Randomly, it dropped to $12.12!!!! Purchased immediately!! It fits my hair dryer perfectly. But, I can tell by looking at it that my hair dryer isn't a standard size on the end so I had to purchase the Bio Ionic brand since that's who makes my hair dryer. Worth it! It says universal, but I would still take measurements to make sure it'll fit yours if it isn't a Bio Ionic brand because they are not ever all universal. The part that attaches to your hair dryer is not a hard plastic- it is rubbery (but firm) so it slides onto your hair dryer but doesn't "click" into place. I think that's why they say it's universal because this allows for some difference in the size of your dryer. But still do your research and compare your hair dryer to the size of the Buttercup hair dryer because it fits it perfectly. You can also twist the diffuser to open up the vents or close them to control the air flow (see pictures). There will always be air flow on the outer ring of the diffuser, but you can completely close off the center, which is nice if you don't want a ton of force. I love it and it was completely worth it!

clean_review_text: I've had it three days now and done several loads. As everyone else said, the hose will not fit a sink in the US. For the first day, I used bowls to fill the machine which was a lot of work but did the job. The next day, I bought a funnel big enough to cover my shower head, drilled two holes on opposite sides at the top, and used a small bungie cord to put a hook into each hole and then hang it over the shower head. I then cut two slits into the hose to allow the funnel to fit inside the faucet end, then duct taped it to seal. I attached the hose to the machine and it worked perfectly. My shower is low flow so I could turn it on full. I put the machine in the shower and ran it from there with no issues, other than to hold the machine when the spinner starts up because the vibration is very strong. My overall reaction is that it does a good job cleaning and the spinner is very good. Similar to a spa's machine to dry your bathing suit. It is labor intensive because you must move the clothes a few at a time to the spinner and you can only put a few things in at a time, but my clothes dry beautifully on a rack and the towels only need 15 minutes in a dryer at the laundromat until I can string up a clothesline in the summer. I did purchase a warranty, though. I'd rather pay $13 than lose $110 if it breaks.

clean_review_title: Decent Gloves Medium Warmth, Do Work On Phones
clean_review_text: When it comes to winter, using phones is sometimes a hassle. Having gloves that make it easier is a big plus. Let's take a look.. WHAT ARE THESE LIKE? These gloves are a gray color and are predominately wool. There is 80% wool, and 20% polymide for stretch and strength. The gloves are not tight, but on the looser side. The fingers are a bit longer than usual, but they have pads on the thumb and first finger OF BOTH HANDS. The description says rhinestones, but I would call it more like tiny marcasite studs instead. That is a hematite looking color, not usually associated with rhinestones. MY GLOVE SIZE My glove size is a medium to large women's. HOW DO THESE PERFORM? These are wool gloves. If you have any sensitivities to wool at all, these might be a bit prickly. They are not soft like merino, but more on the rough side. They do soften up some with gentle hand washing especially when using a fabric softener for wool to soften them up a bit. The finger/thumb pads do work on phones, but because they are slightly longer (as in 1/4 inch) than gloves that usually fit me well, the pads can shift around a bit, making texting or phone/pad use challenging once in a while. The gloves are medium warmth. They are not for really cold frigid days, but they are better than no gloves at all. THE BOTTOM LINE For gloves that fit pretty well, especially if your fingers or finger nails are longer, these gloves are pretty nice. They have pads on both thumbs and fingers and are definitely wool. They are warm for cold weather, but perhaps not frigid weather. MY RATING FOUR STARS. I wish the pads were a little larger so that they did not slide around quite so much. I like that these are REALLY wool instead of a tiny bit, though. They are a lot warmer than synthetic gloves.

clean_review_text: I have very fine thin hair and this is AMAZING! My hair is soft, shiny, bouncy, full, smells great, and feels healthy and clean. If you're hair is greasy, you're not rinsing well or you're using too much as a leave-in conditioner. If your hair is dry, maybe your water is hard, you're not using a bit as leave-in conditioner... All the negative reviews are totally opposite of my experience. Everyone's nose is different. This smells very close to the smell when driving past orange groves in Florida. I look forward to trying the honey peach and the mango coconut. And no one paid me to type this ;) UPDATE: I've been using this product for a couple weeks now. I used to have quite a bit of hair that came out in the shower and also as I was blow drying my hair. I wasn't expecting this and as far as I'm concerned this, in and of itself, is worth the product: my hair has almost completely stopped falling out! Yes, I get hairs that fall out just like everyone else, but before there were times when I wondered how I wasn't bald. Having very thin, fine hair has been difficult because it just looks wispy. I'm excite to see what happens in the next 6 months!

I just wanted to put it out there and let everyone know that I use this product, to supplement my workout routine. As a blogger, I am often provided Press Sample kits by companies for consideration and/or review purposes. Regardless, I only recommend products or services I use personally and believe will be good for others. I refuse to review or endorse products that I have not or will not personally try. Needless to say, I may be a product reviewer but my thoughts are real and none of the things I say on my reviews are made up or fake - they are all based on my experience/s with whatever product I am reviewing. I’m just a regular consumer and I appreciate reading and giving honest feedback about products that regular people like me use on a daily basis or consider buying. My views and opinions are from a customer/end user’s standpoint.

product_title: BIOSKIN Gladiator Knee Brace – Adjustable Hinged Knee Brace for ACL, MCL, LCL, PCL and General Knee Support (XL)
clean_review_title: A superb brace in all regards
clean_review_text: I bought this brace after a fall in which I'd injured my knee, as well as my ankle, and needed to keep my knee completely immobile -- no lateral tuning or twisting at all, and restricted flexing and extension. It was the third brace I ordered -- the first was not nearly strong enough, and the second sizing was off. I'm so glad I found this one: It's a superb brace -- fine, sturdy stays,plus hinges that let you adjust both flexion and extension. I initially thought that latter feature was something I wouldn't need, but it turns out to have made a huge difference during this first month of healing. (Needless to say, I could not have handled a sleeve-type brace -- the open front/wrap style was mandatory, and made my life so much easier.) I've been wearing the brace 24/7 -- night as well as day -- for more than a month and it's been my saving grace. Service has been superb, too: Before I ordered, I measured my thigh, knee and calf; I was on the cusp between sizes, and so ordered up. That seemed like a good idea at the time because it allowed me to wear the brace over my slacks, as well as under -- but after almost a month of continuous wear. the thigh (which was a little larger than I needed to begin with) seemed to have stretched out; I couldn't wrap the upper portion tightly enough and even with the straps cinched tightly, the brace slipped after an hour or two or wear. I wrote the seller and asked if there was a solution -- this brace was costly enough that I didn't want to have to pay out of pocket for a second one -- and they offered to send me a smaller size. It arrived within a few days, accompanied by a label for free return of the first. I couldn't have been more impressed or more grateful. I'd recommend this brace, and this seller, to anyone.
